title: auth — settings
description: Auth exposes tenant-configurable settings for password policy, session lifetime, MFA-required roles, cross-app SSO enablement, new-device notification defaults. Every group registers under the `auth` namespace with `academorix/settings`. Compliance floors (COPPA / GDPR / NIST 800-63B) enforce hard clamps — tenant settings can only TIGHTEN, never loosen.

namespace_registration.defaultValueFactory: Delegates to config('auth.*') when the tenant hasn't overridden. Ensures the effective value falls back to the config layer.
namespace_registration.validator: Enforces compliance floors on password + session + lockout settings. Refuses writes that would reduce policy below NIST 800-63B / OWASP ASVS baselines.

groups.description: Tenant-configurable password requirements. Composes with the framework defaults — tenant can only TIGHTEN.

keys.description: Tenant can increase up to 128; never below the framework floor of 12.
Force rotation after N days. Null = never force. NIST 800-63B advises against; enable only for compliance regimes that require.
Absolute session lifetime. Clamped to auth.session.extended_ttl_days when the tenant holds sanctum_pat_extended_ttl entitlement.
Roles for which MFA enrolment is REQUIRED. Users assigned these roles must enrol MFA within auth.mfa.grace_days_after_enrollment or be blocked from login.
How long a successfully-verified device fingerprint is exempt from MFA re-challenge. 0 = re-challenge on every login.

groups.description: Enable / disable grant-based cross-Application session issuance per tenant. Requires cross_app_sso entitlement.

keys.description: Tenant-level override. Off = the tenant's Users cannot issue cross-app grants even if the global flag is on.

groups.description: Tenant-level defaults for new-device / new-location / impersonation notifications.

keys.description: Cannot be turned off — the impersonated User has a legal right to know. Documented here for tenant admin visibility.

notes: Compliance-critical clamps: min_length cannot go below 12 (NIST 800-63B recommendation). max_age_days cannot be forced below 30 (NIST advises against forced rotation entirely; if enabled, the shortest reasonable cadence is 30 days).
Per-User settings (individual preferences on new-device notifications, for instance) live in `user::user_preferences`, not here. This module owns the TENANT-level policy defaults.
